strategic_context.mission: Navigator's role in the £1M plan: convert LinkedIn reach into school demos and signed contracts
strategic_context.funnel: Wrong Villain post → GUIDE comment → framework DM → device guide → demo → school subscription (£500–£2,000/year)
strategic_context.daily_goal: 5 targeted connection messages to DSLs, Heads, and MAT leads who are decision-makers for the device guide subscription

founder_linkedin_profile.social_proof: Use '7,800 educators follow Justin's Wrong Villain series' as a credibility hook in outreach

outreach_hooks_to_use: β = 0.061 — 46 studies showing social media's effect on mental health is statistically indistinguishable from noise. Evidence-informed DSLs will engage with this.
258 educators saved the last post — use 'your colleagues are using this in staff meetings' as social proof
The device guide: age-unlocking, science-backed, calendar reminders, AI-updated — nobody else has this
Wrong Villain series — 59K impressions, counter-narrative to phone ban consensus. Opens a conversation.

conversion_context.MAT_deal: One MAT sign-up = 5–30 schools. Worth pursuing MAT safeguarding leads over individual schools.
conversion_context.demo_trigger: Get a 20-minute demo booked. That is the only goal of the outreach message.
conversion_context.urgency_hook: Online Safety Act enforcement increasing. Schools need a structured framework now.

target_roles_that_respond: Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL) — highest relevance, cite safeguarding and KCSIE angle
PSHE Lead — strong fit for digital literacy and the Wrong Villain evidence base
MAT Safeguarding Lead — multiplier, covers multiple schools in one conversation
notes_to_self: Every outreach message has one goal: get a 20-minute demo booked. Not to explain the product. Not to share a link. One demo request. The GUIDE funnel does the warming — Navigator converts the warm leads into conversations.
